title: QA: lsp.rs child.wait() after shutdown has no timeout
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/tools/lsp.rs:226-229
## Violation
After issuing `shutdown` + `exit`, `run_lsp_request` calls `child.wait()` with no timeout. A wedged language server will block this call forever — and because `execute_lsp` is synchronous from the tool dispatch loop, the agent stalls.

Quoted (lsp.rs:226-229):
```
    send_lsp_message(&mut stdin, "shutdown", 3, json!(null))?;
    send_lsp_notification(&mut stdin, "exit", json!(null))?;
    let _ = child.wait();
```

`let _` also discards exit status.
## Mandated refactor
1. Spawn a watchdog thread that `child.kill()`s after a bounded shutdown deadline (~2s).
2. Use `wait_timeout` crate / `nix::sys::wait::waitpid(WNOHANG)` polling.
3. Surface the wait result.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
